title: Air-Fryer Tandoori Chicken Breast

subs.name: 400 g paneer slabs or firm tofu — reduce cook time to 8 minutes, flip once
3 tbsp coconut milk + 1 tsp vinegar — same tenderising effect, dairy-free

steps.text: Make diagonal cuts 1 cm deep across each chicken breast on both sides — this helps the marinade penetrate to the centre and gives the char more surface area. Pat the chicken dry with a kitchen cloth.

Whisk together curd, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, garam masala, lemon juice and salt into a smooth marinade. Rub it all over the chicken, pressing well into the cuts. Cover and rest at room temperature for 30 minutes, or refrigerate for up to 8 hours.

Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Lightly brush the basket with oil to prevent sticking. Shake off any excess marinade that might drip and arrange the chicken in a single layer — do not stack.

Air fry at 200°C for 12-15 minutes, flipping once at the halfway mark, until the surface is visibly charred at the edges and the juices run clear when the thickest part is pierced with a skewer. Very thick pieces (over 2 cm) may need 2-3 extra minutes.
Rest the chicken on a board for 5 minutes — this keeps it juicy. Serve with lemon wedges, thinly sliced onion rings and fresh mint or coriander chutney.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → 3 tbsp coconut milk + 1 tsp vinegar — same tenderising effect, dairy-free (works well for lactose-intolerant cooks); chicken → 400 g paneer slabs or firm tofu — reduce cook time to 8 minutes, flip once
